question: Dans un workflow TDD avec agent IA, qui ecrit les tests : l'humain ou l'agent ?

principles.text: Le pattern TDD agent loop preferable : l'humain ecrit le failing test (specifiant l'intent), l'agent itere l'implementation jusqu'a tests verts + tests existants toujours verts. Cette approche beats les alternatives : quand l'agent genere ses propres tests (self-test), la performance chute (TDFlow : 94.3% SWE-Bench Verified avec human tests vs 69.8% avec agent-generated tests). Le final human read of the diff before merge reste obligatoire (bridge avec PICOC #13 SA) — 'no human review per iteration' est valide sur benchmarks mais a qualifier pour production.

sources.name: Han et al. 2025 - TDFlow: Agentic Workflows for Test Driven Development (arXiv:2510.23761)
sources.quote: TDFlow attains 88.8% pass rate on SWE-Bench Lite (an absolute improvement of 27.8% over the next best baseline) and 94.3% on SWE-Bench Verified

sources.name: Han et al. 2025 - TDFlow agent-generated tests performance drop
Mathews & Nadi 2024 - TGen additional remediation loop gains
sources.quote: MBPP: Additional 2.8% improvement through remediation; HumanEval: Additional 3.0% improvement through remediation

sources.name: Naik et al. 2024 - TiCoder (arXiv:2404.10100)
sources.quote: average absolute improvement of 45.97% in the pass@1 code generation accuracy for both datasets and across all LLMs within 5 user interactions

sources.name: Simon Willison - Red/green TDD Agentic Engineering Patterns
SWEBOK v4 KA 5 §7.1 Testing of Emerging Technologies

minus: inconsistency (TDFlow 69.8% with agent tests blurs C1)

etd_balance.benefices: TDFlow 88.8% SWE-Bench Lite, 94.3% Verified (human tests), deterministic stopping
etd_balance.risques: Chute a 69.8% si agent-generated tests - limite self-verification
